2248	/-! ## The affine base-change lemma and its locality reduction -/
2249	
2250	/-- **Affine-local compatibility of the base-change map (locality reduction).** For `f` affine
2251	and `F` quasi-coherent, the base-change map `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` is an isomorphism as soon
2252	as it restricts to an isomorphism on the sections over every affine open of the base `S'`. This is
2253	the locality reduction underlying `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`: it discharges the
2254	affine-open locality criterion `Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_app_affineOpens` for the base-change map,
2255	the per-affine-open hypothesis being the affine–affine section assertion supplied (over each chart)
2256	by the section-level computation. The `[IsAffineHom f]`/`[F.IsQuasicoherent]` hypotheses are
2257	carried because the per-open assertion `H` is only available under them (they make `X` restrict to
2258	`Spec A` and `F` to `M^~` over each affine chart); the reduction itself is the locality criterion.
2259	See blueprint `lem:base_change_map_affine_local`. -/
2260	theorem base_change_map_affine_local (h : IsPullback g' f' f g) [IsAffineHom f]
2261	    (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
2262	    (H : ∀ U : S'.affineOpens, IsIso ((pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F).app U)) :
2263	    IsIso (pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F) :=
2264	  (Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_app_affineOpens (pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F)).mpr H
2265	
2266	/-- **Affine base change.** If `f` is an affine morphism and the square is
2267	cartesian, then the base-change map for the pushforward is an isomorphism. In the
2268	affine-local picture this is the associativity isomorphism
2269	`(R' ⊗_R A) ⊗_A M ≅ R' ⊗_R M`, which needs no flatness.
2270	
2271	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma "Affine base change". -/
2272	theorem affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso (h : IsPullback g' f' f g) [IsAffineHom f]
2273	    (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent] :
2274	    IsIso (pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F) := by
2275	  -- FIRST REDUCTION (locality on `S'`): by `base_change_map_affine_local` it suffices to check
2276	  -- that the base-change map is an isomorphism on the sections over every affine open of `S'`.
2277	  -- This is exactly the locality reduction; the per-affine-open hypothesis is the affine–affine
2278	  -- section assertion that `pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange` is intended to supply.
2279	  apply base_change_map_affine_local f g f' g' h F
2280	  intro U
2281	  -- Remaining goal: `IsIso (Hom.app (pushforwardBaseChangeMap …) U)` for `U` affine.
2282	  --
2283	  -- The two obligations of the blueprint affine close are now NAMED declarations:
2284	  --   • the SECTION-LEVEL identification is `pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange`
2285	  --     (above): in the affine–affine model `Γ(α) = cancelBaseChange⁻¹`, hence an iso (no
2286	  --     flatness). Its statement is in place; its proof (the 4-step generator trace) is the
2287	  --     genuine crux still carrying a `sorry`.
2288	  --   • the SECTION-LEVEL locality reduction is `base_change_map_affine_local` (just applied):
2289	  --     it reduced the global iso to this per-affine-open `U` goal.
2290	  --
2291	  -- WHAT REMAINS HERE (the AFFINE REDUCTION, "obligation 1"): the ambient `S, S', X, X'` are
2292	  -- ARBITRARY (only `f` affine, `F` quasi-coherent). To discharge the per-`U` goal one must
2293	  -- restrict the cartesian square over the affine open `U = Spec R' ⊆ S'` and a chosen affine
2294	  -- `Spec R ⊆ S` containing `g(U)` — over which `[IsAffineHom f]` makes `X` restrict to `Spec A`
2295	  -- and `[F.IsQuasicoherent]` makes `F` restrict to `M^~` — and IDENTIFY `(pushforwardBaseChangeMap
2296	  -- …).app U` with the affine–affine base-change map of the restricted square (blueprint Step 2:
2297	  -- naturality of the adjunction transpose + pushforward-commutes-with-restriction). Then the
2298	  -- per-`U` goal is exactly `pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange` (composed with the
2299	  -- tilde–Γ counit isos to pass `IsIso Γ(α) ⟹ IsIso α` on the QC chart). This restriction-
2300	  -- compatibility of `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` is itself Mathlib-absent and is the remaining
2301	  -- multi-hundred-LOC build for the unconditional general theorem.
2302	  -- See `informal/affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso.md`.
2303	  sorry

2305	/-- **Flat base change, `i = 0` case.** If `g` is flat and `f` is quasi-compact
2306	and quasi-separated, then the base-change map for the pushforward is an
2307	isomorphism. Equivalently, in the affine situation `S = Spec A`, `S' = Spec B`
2308	with `A → B` flat, the comparison map `H⁰(X, F) ⊗_A B → H⁰(X_B, F_B)` is an
2309	isomorphism.

2312	theorem flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso (h : IsPullback g' f' f g) [Flat g]
2313	    [QuasiCompact f] [QuasiSeparated f] (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent] :
2314	    IsIso (pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F) := by

2323	  -- affine-cover infrastructure for `SheafOfModules`; see
2324	  -- `informal/affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso.md`.

1854	  the two legs of the factorization \(g' = b \circ a\) of the inner pullback leg (in the application

1858	  the three-factor composite

1899	\begin{lemma}[Conjugation distributes over composition (reassoc simp lemma)]
1900	  \label{lem:conjugateEquiv_comp_mathlib}
1901	  \lean{CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv_comp}
1902	  \mathlibok
1903	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
1904	  Let \(\mathrm{adj}_1 : L_1 \dashv R_1\), \(\mathrm{adj}_2 : L_2 \dashv R_2\),
1905	  \(\mathrm{adj}_3 : L_3 \dashv R_3\) be adjunctions between the same pair of categories, and let
1906	  \(\alpha : L_2 \Rightarrow L_1\), \(\beta : L_3 \Rightarrow L_2\) be transformations of left adjoints.
1907	  Then the conjugates compose, with order reversed onto the right-adjoint side:
1908	  \[
1909	    \operatorname{conj}(\mathrm{adj}_1,\mathrm{adj}_2)\,\alpha \;\circ\;
1910	    \operatorname{conj}(\mathrm{adj}_2,\mathrm{adj}_3)\,\beta
1911	      \;=\; \operatorname{conj}(\mathrm{adj}_1,\mathrm{adj}_3)\,(\beta \circ \alpha) .
1912	  \]
1913	  Tagged \(\mathtt{@[reassoc (attr := simp)]}\), it is the lead member of the conjugate
1914	  reassociation simp set: it fuses the per-adjunction conjugates of a composite adjunction into the
1915	  conjugate of the composite (the conjugate-side form of the paste-of-mates).
1916	\end{lemma}
1917	
1918	\begin{lemma}[Inverse conjugation distributes over composition (reassoc simp lemma)]
1919	  \label{lem:conjugateEquiv_symm_comp_mathlib}
1920	  \lean{CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv_symm_comp}
1921	  \mathlibok
1922	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
1923	  The inverse-direction companion of Lemma~\ref{lem:conjugateEquiv_comp_mathlib}: for transformations
1924	  \(\alpha : R_1 \Rightarrow R_2\), \(\beta : R_2 \Rightarrow R_3\) of the right adjoints,
1925	  \[
1926	    \operatorname{conj}(\mathrm{adj}_2,\mathrm{adj}_3)^{-1}\beta \;\circ\;
1927	    \operatorname{conj}(\mathrm{adj}_1,\mathrm{adj}_2)^{-1}\alpha
1928	      \;=\; \operatorname{conj}(\mathrm{adj}_1,\mathrm{adj}_3)^{-1}(\alpha \circ \beta) ,
1929	  \]
1930	  also \(\mathtt{@[reassoc (attr := simp)]}\). Together these two close the reassociation bookkeeping of
1931	  the conjugate-side discharge.
1932	\end{lemma}

2272	  \(\gamma_{\psi}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}), and inside the opaque codomain

2278	  whole four-factor chain — for \emph{generic} morphisms \(g', f'\) carrying the cone-leg
2279	  equalities \(g' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\) and
2280	  \(f' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_{R'}\) as explicit hypotheses, in place of the literal
2281	  projections \(\operatorname{pr}_1, \operatorname{pr}_2\). In this abstract form the legs are
2282	  free variables, not terms pinned by the pullback's universal property, so each can be
2283	  eliminated by substituting its defining equation: the equality proof \(w\), the adjunction
2284	  index, and the \(\gamma_{\psi}\)/\(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\) arguments are all expressed in the
2285	  free leg variables and transform uniformly under the substitution. This restatement converts
2286	  the blocked rewrite into a legitimate one; it is the structural heart of the step.
2287	
2288	  \emph{(ii) \(\Gamma\)-collapse of the transparent coherences.} On global sections, read through
2289	  the \(\Gamma\)-pushforward dictionaries (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso},
2290	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}), the two pushforward-composition factors and the
2291	  congruence factor are transparent. The composition coherences collapse to the identity —
2292	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{g',f})^{\mathrm{hom}}\) and
2293	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{f',g})^{\mathrm{inv}}\) evaluate, on the top open, to the identity
2294	  map on restriction of scalars (their section-level values are the identity) — and they
2295	  serve only to rebracket the iterated pushforward along the two factorizations
2296	  \((g' \circ f)_* = (f' \circ g)_*\) of the square's composite. (The
2297	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{g',f})^{\mathrm{hom}}\) collapse is a transparent re-association
2298	  that is most naturally accounted for together with the leg-reindex of step~(iii) rather than at
2299	  this point; the \(\Gamma\)-collapse identity itself is the same.) The congruence factor collapses
2300	  to a canonical transport along the equality of opens: its section-level value
2301	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardCongr}\,w)^{\mathrm{hom}}\) is the
2302	  presheaf-restriction along the canonical equality of opens, i.e.\ a repackaging along
2303	  that equality carrying no substantive content. Thus after the \(\Gamma\)-collapse the only
2304	  surviving non-trivial factor is \(f_{*}\bigl(\eta^{g'}_{\widetilde M}\bigr)\) read on global
2305	  sections.

2313	  \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)\)-unit followed by \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)_{*}\) of the

2669	  each of the three cancellations.
2670	
2671	  \emph{The resolution: congruence lifting.} Each factor-equation is lifted into the ambient
2672	  composite \emph{one neighbour at a time}: a factor identity \(u = u'\) is promoted to
2673	  \(c \circ u = c \circ u'\), or to \(u \circ c = u' \circ c\), by congruence in the composition
2674	  operation with the neighbouring factor \(c\) held fixed, and the promoted steps are chained in
2675	  sequence. The chain is then closed on the definitional bridge between the nested-image and
2676	  composed-functor presentations of the shared object, absorbing the object-form diamond at that
2677	  single closing step. The three atoms
2678	  (Lemmas~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_inner_eCancel_eUnit},
2679	  \ref{lem:base_change_mate_inner_eCancel_pushforwardComp},
2680	  \ref{lem:base_change_mate_inner_eCancel_pullbackComp}) are supplied in the
2681	  \(\Gamma \circ (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_{*}\) form and are spliced directly into the composite
2682	  by this congruence chaining.
2683	
2684	  \emph{The three cancellations.} Cancellation~(1): the \(e\)-unit is invertible
2685	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_inner_eCancel_eUnit}) and meets its inverse inside the codomain
2686	  read. Cancellation~(2): the surviving pushforward-composition coherence has identity
2687	  \(\Gamma\)-image (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_inner_eCancel_pushforwardComp}, whose
2688	  section-level content is the collapse Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaMap_pushforwardComp_hom_eq_id}).
2689	  Cancellation~(3): the trailing pullback-comparison factor cancels the inverse the codomain read
2690	  bakes in (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_inner_eCancel_pullbackComp}). The lone survivor is the
2691	  affine \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)\)-unit, evaluated by Seam~1
2692	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_unit_value}) to \(\rho\).
2693	\end{proof}

2762	
2763	\leanok
2764	[(B) Generator close: the base change of $\rho$ is the inverse regrouping]
2765	  \label{lem:base_change_mate_gstar_generator_close}
2766	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_gstar_generator_close}
2767	  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv, def:base_change_mate_inner_value}
2768	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma `lemma-affine-base-change'
2769	  % (\S\,Cohomology and base change, I), proof, the "boils down to the equality" step
2770	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L933--938).
2771	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Thus we see that the lemma boils down to the equality
2772	  % $$ (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M = R' \otimes_R M $$ as $R'$-modules."
2773	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma ``Affine base change''
2774	  (proof, ``boils down to the equality'' step).}
2775	  Let \(\rho : M \to (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\), \(m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\), be the
2776	  inner comparison value of Definition~\ref{def:base_change_mate_inner_value}. The extension of
2777	  scalars along \(\psi : R \to R'\) of \(\rho\), post-composed with the algebraic counit
2778	  \(\varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}}\) of the extension/restriction-of-scalars adjunction along \(\psi\),
2779	  is an \(R'\)-linear map \(R' \otimes_R M \to (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\); on the generator
2780	  \(r' \otimes m\) it returns \(r' \cdot ((1 \otimes 1) \otimes m) = (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\),
2781	  which is exactly the value of the inverse regrouping isomorphism
2782	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}) on \(r' \otimes m\). Both maps being
2783	  \(R'\)-linear and agreeing on the generators \(r' \otimes m\), they coincide:
2784	  \[
2785	    \operatorname{ext}_\psi(\rho) \,\circ\, \varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}}
2786	      \;=\; \operatorname{regroup}^{-1}.
2787	  \]
2788	\end{lemma}
2789	\begin{proof}
2790	  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv, def:base_change_mate_inner_value}
2791	  Both sides are \(R'\)-linear maps out of \(R' \otimes_R M\), so by extensionality it suffices to
2792	  check the value on the generators \(r' \otimes m\). The left side sends
2793	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto r' \cdot \rho(m) = r' \cdot ((1 \otimes 1) \otimes m) =
2794	  (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\), using that \(\varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}}\) reads the extension of
2795	  scalars by acting through the \(R'\)-scalar. The inverse regrouping isomorphism of
2796	  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv} sends, by definition,
2797	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\) (in the \(A \otimes_R R'\) orientation). The two
2798	  values agree on every generator, so the maps are equal. No flatness hypothesis is used.
2799	\end{proof}

3046	  \(\varepsilon_g\), with no opaque transpose remaining,
3047	  \(\Gamma(\theta) = \Gamma\bigl(g^*(\theta_{\mathrm{in}})\bigr) \circ \Gamma(\varepsilon_g)\).
3048	  Substituting this factorization, the conjugated map
3049	  \[

3051	      = \Theta_{\mathrm{src}}^{-1} \circ

3056	  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_gstar_transpose}, which states it equals

3061	  equality'' \((A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M = R' \otimes_R M\) of the source, with no
3062	  flatness hypothesis used anywhere.

3286	  \medskip

3288	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens} it suffices to prove that the
3289	  base-change map of Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map} is an
3290	  isomorphism on sections over every affine open of \(S'\). This is exactly the
3291	  locality reduction Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_map_affine_local}: restricting the
3292	  cartesian square to an affine open \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(R') \subseteq S'\) and
3293	  a chosen affine \(\operatorname{Spec}(R) \subseteq S\) containing \(g(U)\), the
3294	  hypothesis \([\mathrm{IsAffineHom}\ f]\) makes \(X\) restrict to

3301	  model, where the base-change map is a morphism \(\alpha\) of quasi-coherent
3302	  \((\operatorname{Spec} R')\)-modules.

3312	  \[

3330	    \operatorname{cancelBaseChange} :

3332	    \qquad (r' \otimes a) \otimes m \;\mapsto\; r' \otimes (a \cdot m)
3333	  \]
3334	  of \(R'\)-modules (Lemma~\ref{lem:cancelBaseChange_mathlib}). This is the entire
3335	  content of the affine close, and it is performed \emph{directly on the module of
3336	  global sections} — there is no separate abstract adjoint-mate identification at the
3337	  level of sheaves. Because \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\) is an isomorphism with
3338	  no flatness hypothesis whatsoever, \(\Gamma(\alpha)\) is an isomorphism, hence so is
3339	  \(\alpha\) on the affine chart, and by the locality reduction so is the global
3340	  base-change map. Flatness of \(g\) plays no role in the affine lemma; it enters only
3341	  in the global statement Theorem~\ref{thm:flat_base_change_pushforward}, where it is
3342	  used to commute \(- \otimes_A B\) with the finite equalizer computing \(H^0\).

3652	    \(A\)-module \(\Gamma(S, f_*\mathcal{F}) = \Gamma(X,\mathcal{F})\); likewise
3653	    \(f'_*\mathcal{F}'\) is associated to \(\Gamma(X_B,\mathcal{F}_B)\). Pullback along
3654	    \(g\) corresponds to \(- \otimes_A B\) on modules, so under the
3655	    tilde-equivalence the sheaf base-change map is the tilde of the comparison map
3656	    \(\Gamma(X,\mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \to \Gamma(X_B,\mathcal{F}_B)\); since
3657	    \(\widetilde{(-)}\) is fully faithful on quasi-coherent modules, one is an
3658	    isomorphism iff the other is.
3659	  \end{proof}
3660	\end{lemma}

3662	\begin{theorem}\leanok
3663	[Flat base change, $i=0$ case]
3664	  \label{thm:flat_base_change_pushforward}
3665	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso}
3666	  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:affine_base_change_pushforward,
3667	        lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib,
3668	        lem:flat_base_change_reduce_global_sections,
3669	        lem:flat_base_change_mayer_vietoris}

3672	  % references/stacks-coherent.md).
3673	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Consider a cartesian diagram of schemes
3674	  % $$
3675	  % \xymatrix{
3676	  % X' \ar[d]_{f'} \ar[r]_{g'} & X \ar[d]^f \\
3677	  % S' \ar[r]^g & S
3678	  % }
3679	  % $$
3680	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module
3681	  % with pullback $\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}$.
3682	  % Assume that $g$ is flat and that $f$ is quasi-compact and quasi-separated.
3683	  % For any $i \geq 0$
3684	  % \begin{enumerate}
3685	  % \item the base change map of
3686	  % Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-base-change-map-flat-case}
3687	  % is an isomorphism
3688	  % $$
3689	  % g^*R^if_*\mathcal{F} \longrightarrow R^if'_*\mathcal{F}',
3690	  % $$
3691	  % \item if $S = \Spec(A)$ and $S' = \Spec(B)$, then
3692	  % $H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B = H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')$.
3693	  % \end{enumerate}"
3694	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Tag 02KH (``Flat base change''), the
3695	  \(i = 0\) case.}
3696	  Consider the cartesian square
3697	  \[
3698	    \begin{array}{ccc}
3699	      X' & \xrightarrow{\;g'\;} & X \\[2pt]
3700	      {\scriptstyle f'}\big\downarrow & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle f} \\[2pt]
3701	      S' & \xrightarrow{\;g\;} & S
3702	    \end{array}
3703	  \]
3704	  with \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module and
3705	  \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\). Assume that \(g\) is flat and that \(f\)
3706	  is quasi-compact and quasi-separated. Then the base-change map of
3707	  Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map} is an isomorphism
3708	  \[
3709	    g^*\bigl(f_*\mathcal{F}\bigr) \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
3710	    f'_*\bigl((g')^*\mathcal{F}\bigr) = f'_*\mathcal{F}' .
3711	  \]
3712	  Equivalently, in the affine situation \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\),
3713	  \(S' = \operatorname{Spec}(B)\) with \(A \to B\) flat, the comparison map
3714	  \[
3715	    H^0(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
3716	    H^0(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B)
3717	  \]
3718	  is an isomorphism, where \(X_B = X \times_{\operatorname{Spec}(A)}
3719	  \operatorname{Spec}(B)\) and \(\mathcal{F}_B\) is the pullback of
3720	  \(\mathcal{F}\).
3721	
3722	  The hypothesis that \(\mathcal{F}\) is quasi-coherent is essential: it is what
3723	  makes \(f_*\mathcal{F}\) quasi-coherent on the affine base and thus the tilde of
3724	  a module, and it is what lets the affine reduction
3725	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_base_change_pushforward}) write \(\mathcal{F}\) as
3726	  \(\widetilde{M}\) over each affine open. Without it the comparison map need not be
3727	  an isomorphism.
3728	\end{theorem}

3800	  For \(i = 0\) the argument is {\v C}ech-\emph{free}: it uses only the sheaf
3801	  condition (the degree-\(0\)/\(1\) part of {\v C}ech, i.e.\ an equalizer of a finite
3802	  cover), the affine lemma, and the fact that flat base change commutes with that
3803	  finite equalizer. There is no Leray sequence and no {\v C}ech-to-cohomology spectral
3804	  sequence. The proof is the composite of the decomposition chain.
3805	
3806	  \medskip
3807	  \emph{Reduction to the global-sections comparison.} By
3808	  Lemma~\ref{lem:flat_base_change_reduce_global_sections}, the base-change map of
3809	  Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map} is an isomorphism if and only if,
3810	  after reducing to affine bases \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\),
3811	  \(S' = \operatorname{Spec}(B)\) with \(A \to B\) flat, the module comparison map
3812	  \[
3813	    \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \longrightarrow \Gamma(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B),
3814	    \qquad X_B = X \times_{\operatorname{Spec}(A)} \operatorname{Spec}(B),
3815	  \]
3816	  is an isomorphism (this uses that \(f_*\mathcal{F}\) is quasi-coherent — \(f\) is
3817	  quasi-compact and quasi-separated — so each side is the tilde of its module of
3818	  global sections, and that pullback along \(g\) is \(- \otimes_A B\) on modules).
3819	
3820	  \medskip
3821	  \emph{The comparison map is an isomorphism.} Since \(f\) is quasi-compact and
3822	  quasi-separated, \(X\) is quasi-compact and quasi-separated over \(A\), so
3823	  Lemma~\ref{lem:flat_base_change_mayer_vietoris} applies and gives
3824	  \[
3825	    \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; \Gamma(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B).
3826	  \]
3827	  Internally that lemma presents \(\Gamma(X,\mathcal{F})\) as the finite
3828	  sheaf-condition equalizer of a finite affine cover
3829	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:gamma_finite_equalizer}), base changes the equalizer diagram term by
3830	  term via the affine lemma Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_base_change_pushforward}
3831	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_changed_equalizer_diagram}), and commutes \(- \otimes_A B\)
3832	  past that finite equalizer by flatness
3833	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}), reducing the general
3834	  quasi-separated case to the separated case
3835	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:flat_base_change_separated}) by Mayer--Vietoris induction on the
3836	  number of cover members. Combined with the reduction above, the sheaf-level
3837	  base-change map is an isomorphism.
3838	\end{proof}

3840	\subsection{The \texorpdfstring{$A$}{A}-module \texorpdfstring{$\operatorname{eqLocus}$}{eqLocus} globalization (FBC-B)}
3841	
3842	The separated-case argument is repackaged here at the level of \(A\)-modules, where
3843	\(A = \Gamma(X,\mathcal{O}_X)\) is the ring of global sections of the structure sheaf, so that flat
3844	base change becomes the Mathlib equivalence \(\operatorname{tensorEqLocusEquiv}\)
3845	(Lemma~\ref{lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}) applied to an honest
3846	\(\operatorname{LinearMap.eqLocus}\). The blocks below construct that presentation concretely: every
3847	module of sections \(\Gamma(U, M)\) is viewed as an \(A\)-module by restriction of scalars along the
3848	structure-sheaf restriction \(A \to \Gamma(U,\mathcal{O}_X)\), the restriction maps of \(M\) become
3849	\(A\)-linear, and the two product legs of the sheaf condition assemble into a pair of \(A\)-linear maps
3850	whose equalizer locus is \(\Gamma(X, M)\). Throughout, \(M : X.\mathrm{Modules}\) is a sheaf of
3851	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules and \(U : \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) is an open cover with
3852	\(\bigvee_i U_i = \top\).
3853	
3854	The bijectivity of the keystone presentation rests on two element-level sheaf axioms of the underlying
3855	abelian-group sheaf \(M.\mathrm{presheaf}\), recorded as Mathlib dependency anchors.
3856	
3857	\begin{lemma}[Sheaf separatedness: local equality forces global equality]
3858	  \label{lem:sheaf_eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib}
3859	  \lean{TopCat.Sheaf.eq_of_locally_eq'}
3860	  \mathlibok
3861	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
3862	  Let \(\mathcal{G}\) be a sheaf on a topological space \(X\), \(U : \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) a
3863	  family covering an open \(V\) (i.e.\ \(V \le \bigvee_i U_i\)), and \(s, t \in \mathcal{G}(V)\) two
3864	  sections. If the restrictions of \(s\) and \(t\) to every \(U_i\) agree, then \(s = t\). This is the
3865	  separatedness half of the sheaf condition, stated element-wise.
3866	\end{lemma}
3867	
3868	\begin{lemma}[Sheaf gluing: a compatible family has a unique amalgamation]
3869	  \label{lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}
3870	  \lean{TopCat.Sheaf.existsUnique_gluing'}
3871	  \mathlibok
3872	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
3873	  Let \(\mathcal{G}\) be a sheaf on \(X\), \(U : \iota \to \mathrm{Opens}(X)\) a family covering an open
3874	  \(V\), and \((s_i)_i\) a family of sections \(s_i \in \mathcal{G}(U_i)\) that is \emph{compatible}
3875	  (the restrictions of \(s_i\) and \(s_j\) to \(U_i \cap U_j\) agree for all \(i, j\)). Then there is a
3876	  unique \(s \in \mathcal{G}(V)\) whose restriction to each \(U_i\) is \(s_i\). This is the gluing half
3877	  of the sheaf condition, stated element-wise.
3878	\end{lemma}

3880	\begin{definition}\leanok
3881	[The ground ring of global sections]
3882	  \label{def:fbcb_groundRing}
3883	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.groundRing}
3884	  For a scheme \(X\), the \emph{ground ring} is the commutative ring
3885	  \(A = \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X) = \mathcal{O}_X(\top)\) of global sections of the structure sheaf,
3886	  taken from the \(\mathbf{CommRing}\)-valued structure presheaf so its ring and algebra structures
3887	  resolve canonically.
3888	\end{definition}

3890	\begin{definition}\leanok
3891	[The structure-sheaf restriction ring map \texorpdfstring{$A \to \Gamma(U,\mathcal{O}_X)$}{A to sections over U}]
3892	  \label{def:fbcb_rhoU}
3893	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rhoU}
3894	  \uses{def:fbcb_groundRing}
3895	  For an open \(U \subseteq X\), the structure-sheaf restriction along \(U \subseteq \top\) is a ring
3896	  homomorphism \(\rho_U : A = \Gamma(X,\mathcal{O}_X) \to \Gamma(U,\mathcal{O}_X)\). It is the ring map
3897	  along which sections over \(U\) are regarded as \(A\)-modules.
3898	\end{definition}

3900	\begin{lemma}\leanok
3901	[Transitivity of the restriction ring maps]
3902	  \label{lem:fbcb_rhoU_comp}
3903	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rhoU_comp}
3904	  \uses{def:fbcb_rhoU}
3905	  For opens \(V \le U \subseteq X\), the structure-sheaf restriction
3906	  \(\Gamma(U,\mathcal{O}_X) \to \Gamma(V,\mathcal{O}_X)\) composed with \(\rho_U\) equals \(\rho_V\):
3907	  \[
3908	    \bigl(\Gamma(U,\mathcal{O}_X) \to \Gamma(V,\mathcal{O}_X)\bigr) \circ \rho_U = \rho_V .
3909	  \]
3910	  \begin{proof}
3911	    \uses{def:fbcb_rhoU}
3912	    Both sides are the structure-sheaf restriction \(\Gamma(X,\mathcal{O}_X) \to \Gamma(V,\mathcal{O}_X)\)
3913	    along \(V \subseteq \top\), factored as \(V \subseteq U \subseteq \top\); functoriality of the
3914	    structure presheaf collapses the two-step restriction to the one-step restriction.
3915	  \end{proof}
3916	\end{lemma}

3949	\begin{lemma}\leanok

4018	\begin{lemma}\leanok
4019	[The restricted family is compatible]
4020	  \label{lem:fbcb_leftRes_toCover}
4021	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.leftRes_toCover}
4022	  \uses{def:fbcb_leftRes, def:fbcb_rightRes, def:fbcb_toCover, lem:fbcb_gammaResA_comp}
4023	  For every global section \(s \in \Gamma(\top, M)\) the two legs agree on the restricted family:
4024	  \[
4025	    \mathrm{leftRes}\bigl(\mathrm{toCover}(s)\bigr) = \mathrm{rightRes}\bigl(\mathrm{toCover}(s)\bigr).

4027	  \begin{proof}

838	is the honest cut of the affine close: locality on \(S'\), then a pure computation on
839	the module of global sections. No separate abstract adjoint-mate identification is
840	performed at the level of sheaves; the mate is unwound \emph{on sections}, where it
841	becomes \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\) directly.
842	
843	\begin{lemma}
844	
845	\leanok
846	[Affine-local compatibility of the base-change map]
847	  \label{lem:base_change_map_affine_local}
848	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_map_affine_local}
849	  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens}
850	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
851	  % `lemma-affine-base-change' (\S\,Cohomology and base change, I), proof, the
852	  % "local on $S$ and $S'$" step (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
853	  % L920--926).
854	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "The statement is local on $S$ and $S'$. Hence we may
855	  % assume $X = \Spec(A)$, $S = \Spec(R)$,
856	  % $S' = \Spec(R')$ and $\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}$
857	  % for some $A$-module $M$."
858	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma ``Affine base
859	  change'' (proof, local-on-\(S\)-and-\(S'\) step).}
860	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be an affine morphism and \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
861	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, with a base-change square as above. The formation of the
862	  base-change map (Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map}) is compatible
863	  with restriction to affine opens of \(S'\): for each affine open \(U \subseteq S'\), the
864	  restriction of the base-change map to sections over \(U\) agrees with the
865	  base-change map of the square obtained by restricting along \(U\). Consequently,
866	  by the affine-open locality criterion
867	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens}, the assertion that the base-change
868	  map is an isomorphism over an arbitrary base-change square \((S, S', X, X')\)
869	  reduces to the affine--affine case \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(R)\),
870	  \(S' = \operatorname{Spec}(R')\), \(X = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\),
871	  \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\) for an \(A\)-module \(M\).
872	
873	\end{lemma}
874	
875	\begin{proof}
876	  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens}
877	  By the affine-open locality criterion
878	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens}, the base-change map is an isomorphism
879	  of \(\mathcal{O}_{S'}\)-modules as soon as its restriction to the sections over each
880	  affine open \(U \subseteq S'\) is an isomorphism. The only content beyond the bare criterion is to identify that
881	  restriction with the base-change map of the affine--affine square cut out over
882	  \(U\). We obtain the identification by unfolding the definition of the map and
883	  pushing the restriction functor through each of its building blocks. The
884	  compatibility this needs is \emph{not} a new coherence theorem: it is the naturality
885	  of the adjunction transpose, together with the standard fact that pushforward
886	  commutes with restriction to an open of the base. We spell this out in three steps.
